signals.headline: Nuclear Program Remains Central Sticking Point | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Nuclear Program Remains Central Sticking Point | YES | HIGH confidence**

Vice President JD Vance confirmed Trump's core negotiating position: the US will "make Iran thrive" if Iran commits to "not having a nuclear weapon"[1]. Trump rejected reports claiming the US demanded 20-year uranium enrichment suspensions, instead insisting Iran must not possess nuclear weapons with no fixed time limits[2].

Ceasefire Status Holding | YES | HIGH confidence**

The two-week ceasefire announced April 7 remains in effect, with Iran having reopened the Strait of Hormuz and ceased "defensive operations"[4]. However, sporadic attacks underscore the fragility of this calm[1].

Trump declared in a Fox Business interview that the Iran war "is very close to over" and stated "I view it as very close to over," adding that he believes Iran "wants to make a deal very badly"[1].
